expected_failure_modes: Vehicle may stay high to collect altitude progress without approaching target.
Agent may oscillate tilt to exploit stability component's tolerance.
Excessive engine use may be traded for safety, leading to high fuel cost but still achieving terminal reward.
Crash landing might satisfy leg contact but fail due to high velocity or tilt; event condition may be too strict.
risk_notes: No explicit penalty for hard landings unless tilt or y_vel thresholds exceeded at contact.
Control cost weight may need tuning relative to progress rewards.
Exponential decay for altitude gives diminishing returns at low heights, may not incentivize final touchdown.
